description: Trigger discrimination for the error/exception-path security REVIEW lens (this skill) vs broad diff security review (security-pr-reviewer), building a named control (appsec-implementer), triaging scanner output (static-analysis-reviewer), and designing the error model (error-taxonomy-designer).

cases.prompt: Go through our exception handling and fallbacks — do we fail open anywhere, does anything skip an auth check when it throws, and do our error responses leak internals?
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — the specific security lens on error/exception handling is this skill: fail-closed, error-path authz, exception bypass, leak-free responses.
cases.reason: Reviewing how the code behaves under exceptional conditions, security-wise, is this skill's core scope — not a whole-diff gate, not building the fix, not the error model.

cases.prompt: Security-review this PR before merge — it touches auth, a new upload endpoint, and some config.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — the broad security gate on an actual diff is security-pr-reviewer; it may compose this skill's lens when the diff's risk is in error paths.
cases.reason: security-pr-reviewer owns the severity-ranked security review of a whole change. This skill is the deep single lens on error/exception handling, invoked directly or composed within that gate.

cases.prompt: Make this permission check fail closed — implement the deny-on-exception behavior with a negative test proving it.
cases.reason: Implementing a named, decided control test-first is appsec-implementer (manual-only, side-effecting). This skill produces the finding and the fix direction; it never edits code.

cases.prompt: Our SAST tool flagged 40 'improper exception handling' findings — triage which are real and rank them.
cases.reason: Sorting scanner findings into true/false positives and ranking them is static-analysis-reviewer. This skill reviews the actual code, not another tool's output.

cases.prompt: Design our API's error taxonomy — the error codes, the response envelope, retryability semantics, and how errors map across layers.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the error MODEL (codes, envelope, semantics) is error-taxonomy-designer; this skill reviews whether the HANDLING is secure, whatever the model.
cases.reason: Designing what errors ARE is error-taxonomy-designer. Reviewing what error paths DO to security (fail-open, bypass, leaks) is this skill.
